𝗚𝗘𝗡𝗥𝗘𝗖𝗢𝗡 | To reconstruct detailed and editable 3D meshes of indoor scenes from multiple 2D images by combining generative modeling with scene decomposition.  🔥 Super Hot!

⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🐍 Python 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: To reconstruct detailed and editable 3D meshes of indoor scenes from multiple 2D images by combining generative modeling with scene decomposition.
📅 Created: May 2026 • Updated: Jul 2026

⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ High-fidelity 3D scene reconstruction from multi-view images
✅ Integration of strong generative 3D priors (e.g., Trellis.2)
✅ Scene reconstruction via spatially-localized, overlapping chunks
✅ Projection-based conditioning mechanism for 3D feature lifting
✅ Generation of faithful, editable PBR mesh reconstructions
✅ Outperforms cutting-edge reconstruction methods


⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐ 472 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 35 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 3 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

GenRecon is a Python-based framework for high-fidelity 3D scene reconstruction from multi-view RGB images. It leverages strong generative 3D priors, specifically Trellis.2, by conditioning the generation process on localized scene chunks to achieve scene-scale reconstruction. The approach enables faithful, editable PBR mesh reconstructions of indoor environments, outperforming existing methods.

⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆

torlink is a terminal-based torrent finder and downloader that simplifies the process of locating and downloading torrent files. It searches a curated list of reputable sources, presents results clearly, and handles downloads directly within the terminal, offering a zero-setup, zero-configuration user experience.

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

L0p4Map is a professional network monitoring and visualization tool designed for security researchers. It combines high-speed ARP scanning, hostname resolution, device fingerprinting, and role detection with a comprehensive nmap integration for deep network analysis and vulnerability assessment.

𝗖𝗢𝗗𝗘𝗕𝗔𝗦𝗘-𝗠𝗘𝗠𝗢𝗥𝗬-𝗠𝗖𝗣 | To provide AI coding agents with fast, efficient, and comprehensive code intelligence by indexing codebases into a navigable knowledge graph.  🔥 Super Hot!

◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 💻 C 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: To provide AI coding agents with fast, efficient, and comprehensive code intelligence by indexing codebases into a navigable knowledge graph.
📅 Created: Feb 2026 • Updated: Jul 2026

◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ High-performance code indexing (milliseconds for average repo, minutes for Linux kernel)
✅ Persistent knowledge graph creation
✅ Sub-millisecond query response times
✅ Support for 158 languages via tree-sitter AST analysis
✅ Hybrid LSP semantic type resolution for enhanced accuracy
✅ Single static binary, zero dependencies
✅ Built-in 3D graph visualization UI
✅ Plug-and-play compatibility with 11 coding agents


◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ 25.7K 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 1.9K 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 145 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Codebase-Memory-MCP is a high-performance C-based code intelligence engine that indexes entire codebases into a knowledge graph for AI coding agents. It achieves extreme indexing speeds and sub-millisecond query times, supporting 158 languages with enhanced semantic resolution for popular ones, and ships as a single, zero-dependency static binary.

◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇

𝗥𝗟𝗠 | To provide a general plug-and-play inference library for Recursive Language Models (RLMs), enabling language models to process and interact with extremely long contexts by recursively calling themselves within a sandboxed environment.  📈 Growing!

⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🐍 Python 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: To provide a general plug-and-play inference library for Recursive Language Models (RLMs), enabling language models to process and interact with extremely long contexts by recursively calling themselves within a sandboxed environment.
📅 Created: Dec 2025 • Updated: Jul 2026

⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Task-agnostic inference paradigm for language models
✅ Handles near-infinite length contexts
✅ Programmatically examines, decomposes, and recursively calls itself
✅ Replaces canonical llm.completion with rlm.completion
✅ Offloads context as a variable in a REPL environment
✅ Supports interaction with and launching of sub-LM calls
✅ Extensible inference engine
✅ Training environment for RLMs
✅ Support for various sandbox environments
✅ Compatibility with standard API-based and local LLMs


⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐⭐ 5.2K 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 848 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 34 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Recursive Language Models (RLMs) offer a novel inference paradigm for language models, enabling them to handle near-infinite context lengths by programmatically examining, decomposing, and recursively calling themselves. This library provides an extensible inference engine and training environment for RLMs, supporting various sandboxes and standard API-based or local LLMs.

⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/alexzhang13/rlm

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🦀 Rust 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Removing unwanted system applications (bloatware) from Android devices to enhance privacy, security, and performance, without requiring root access.
📅 Created: Oct 2023 • Updated: Jul 2026

◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Cross-platform GUI application
✅ Uses ADB for debloating
✅ Supports non-rooted devices
✅ Improves privacy, security, and battery life
✅ Reduces attack surface by removing system apps
✅ Fetches package lists from GitHub
✅ Checks for updates


◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐⭐ 8.3K 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 350 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 241 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Universal Android Debloater Next Generation (UAD-ng) is a cross-platform GUI application written in Rust that uses ADB to remove pre-installed system apps (bloatware) from non-rooted Android devices. It aims to improve device privacy, security, and performance by reducing the attack surface and freeing up resources.
